info.description: Affinity API for software platforms connecting practices to the compounder network. A practice is the customer organization, a provider is an individual clinician or prescriber, and a location is a physical practice site. The API covers practice management, catalog discovery, prescription-order submission, fulfillment tracking, and webhooks.
info.title: Affinity API

get.description: Returns the platform organization and the current role.
Returns the subject, mode, and scopes for the API key.
Lists the catalog items that are eligible for the authenticated account and mode.

post.description: Creates an editable test draft. In live mode, it releases an existing signed prescription version.

patch.description: Updates a test order that is in the draft state.

post.description: Cancels an order before shipment. To correct an order, cancel it and create a replacement.
Checks a test draft and submits it to the test review queue.
Creates a practice for the platform. Send Idempotency-Key when you retry the same request.

get.description: Returns one practice that belongs to the platform.

patch.description: Updates one practice that belongs to the platform. Send Idempotency-Key when you retry the same request.

AffinityVersion.description: The effective dated Affinity API contract version.

RequestId.description: Identifier used to correlate a request with Affinity support and logs.

AffinityVersion.description: Optional per-request override for the service account's pinned API version.
AffinityVersion.name: Affinity-Version

IdempotencyKey.description: Unique operation key required for every mutation.
IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key

affinityApiKey.description: Server-side Affinity service key. Use this header when bearer authorization is unavailable.
affinityApiKey.name: x-affinity-api-key

bearerAuth.description: Server-side Affinity service key using the Bearer authentication scheme.
